meta.revisionNote: Revision 2 (June 23). Athlete ahead of plan after 3 strong weeks — skipped re-base, running 4x/week, HR down ~20bpm at same pace. Paces recalibrated faster; long-run ramp accelerated; now 24 weeks from current week.

foundation.notes: 3 weeks of consistent training logged. Adapted faster than expected — graduated out of re-base.

currentForm.consistency: Strong — 11 runs in 3 weeks, no gaps
currentForm.notes: Easy pace ~6:30/km at HR 140. Same pace as May baseline but 20 bpm lower HR — clear aerobic gains. Knee symptom-free. Added cycling + walks as cross-training.

strengths.evidence: HR dropped from 159→139 at same 6:30/km pace over 3 weeks
Good long-run pacing instinct (ran 6km at 7:41/km, kept HR 141)

limiters.evidence: Longest run still only 6 km — endurance is the gap to close
Strength work only done occasionally — knee insurance is under-used
Recovery constrained by 1yo + work; 4 runs/week leaves little margin

constraints: Knee currently symptom-free — keep it that way

hr.notes: Refined from 11 logged runs. Max HR 180 seen at hard effort; true max ~185–190. Validate Z4/Z5 with a 5K time trial around week 6.

pace.notes: Recalibrated UP from your logged data. Long runs slower than easy runs on purpose. Goal pace held conservative until your 18–22km runs prove what you can sustain — likely revisit down then.

phases.name: Base
phases.focus: Aerobic base + long run to 13 km

phases.name: Build
phases.focus: Tempo + MP work, long run to 22 km

workouts.name: Strength B — Core & Stability
workouts.description: Core + single-leg balance for running economy and knee tracking.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): cat-cow, bird-dog 2x8
Main (3 rounds, 45s rest):
  • Plank — 30–45s
  • Side plank — 20–30s each side
  • Dead bug — 8 reps each side
  • Single-leg deadlift (no weight) — 8 reps each leg
  • Step-ups onto chair — 10 each leg
Cool-down (5 min): pigeon stretch, child's pose

workouts.name: Easy + 4 Strides — 35 min
workouts.description: Easy aerobic with short pickups to keep leg speed sharp.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + 10 min easy
Main: 10 min easy (6:30–7:00/km)
Strides: 4 x 20s acceleration to ~5K pace on flat, 60s walk between
Cool-down: 5 min walk

workouts.name: Easy Run — 30 min (optional 4th run)
workouts.description: Optional. Run it if recovery is good — skip without guilt if tired or short on sleep.
workouts.humanReadable:
OPTIONAL 4th run — only if you feel fresh.
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 20 min very easy (HR <145, pace 6:45–7:15/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk

If knee feels anything, or sleep was bad, do mobility instead.

workouts.name: Easy Run — 35 min
workouts.description: Easy/conversational. Keep HR under 150. This pace should feel comfortable.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + light jog
Main: 25 min easy (HR 135–150, pace 6:30–7:00/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretch

workouts.name: Strength A — Lower Body Foundation
workouts.description: Knee insurance. Single-leg & glute work. Non-negotiable now that long runs are growing.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): leg swings, hip circles, glute bridges 2x10
Main (3 rounds, 60s rest):
  • Bodyweight squats — 12 reps (slow & controlled)
  • Reverse lunges — 8 reps each leg
  • Single-leg glute bridges — 10 reps each leg
  • Calf raises — 15 reps
  • Side-lying clamshells — 12 reps each side
Cool-down (5 min): hip flexor & quad stretches

workouts.name: Long Run — 7 km
workouts.description: The engine of your marathon. Keep it SLOW — slower than your easy runs. Time on feet matters more than pace.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 7 km easy (7:00–7:30/km, HR 135–150). Deliberately slow.
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretches
Fuel: After 75 min, 30g carbs (gel/banana). Water every 15 min.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

workouts.name: Strength B — Core & Stability

workouts.description: The engine of your marathon. Keep it SLOW — slower than your easy runs. Time on feet matters more than pace.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 16 km easy (7:00–7:30/km, HR 135–150). Deliberately slow.
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretches
Fuel: After 75 min, 30g carbs (gel/banana). Water every 15 min.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

workouts.name: Easy Run — 40 min
workouts.description: Easy/conversational. Keep HR under 150. This pace should feel comfortable.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + light jog
Main: 30 min easy (HR 135–150, pace 6:30–7:00/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretch

workouts.name: Mobility & Recovery
workouts.description: Light movement — supports recovery.
workouts.humanReadable:
10–15 min total:
  • Foam roll quads, calves, glutes (5 min)
  • Hip flexor stretch — 60s each side
  • Hamstring stretch — 60s each side
  • Ankle circles & calf stretch — 30s each

workouts.name: Easy Run — 45 min
workouts.description: Easy/conversational. Keep HR under 150. This pace should feel comfortable.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + light jog
Main: 35 min easy (HR 135–150, pace 6:30–7:00/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretch

workouts.name: Strength A — Lower Body Foundation
workouts.description: Knee insurance. Single-leg & glute work. Non-negotiable now that long runs are growing.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): leg swings, hip circles, glute bridges 2x10
Main (3 rounds, 60s rest):
  • Bodyweight squats — 12 reps (slow & controlled)
  • Reverse lunges — 8 reps each leg
  • Single-leg glute bridges — 10 reps each leg
  • Calf raises — 15 reps
  • Side-lying clamshells — 12 reps each side
Cool-down (5 min): hip flexor & quad stretches

workouts.name: Long Run — 12 km
workouts.description: The engine of your marathon. Keep it SLOW — slower than your easy runs. Time on feet matters more than pace.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 12 km easy (7:00–7:30/km, HR 135–150). Deliberately slow.
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretches
Fuel: After 75 min, 30g carbs (gel/banana). Water every 15 min.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

workouts.name: Strength B — Core & Stability
workouts.description: Core + single-leg balance for running economy and knee tracking.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): cat-cow, bird-dog 2x8
Main (3 rounds, 45s rest):
  • Plank — 30–45s
  • Side plank — 20–30s each side
  • Dead bug — 8 reps each side
  • Single-leg deadlift (no weight) — 8 reps each leg
  • Step-ups onto chair — 10 each leg
Cool-down (5 min): pigeon stretch, child's pose

workouts.name: Easy Run — 50 min
workouts.description: Easy/conversational. Keep HR under 150. This pace should feel comfortable.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + light jog
Main: 40 min easy (HR 135–150, pace 6:30–7:00/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretch

workouts.name: Easy Run — 40 min (optional 4th run)
workouts.description: Optional. Run it if recovery is good — skip without guilt if tired or short on sleep.
workouts.humanReadable:
OPTIONAL 4th run — only if you feel fresh.
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 30 min very easy (HR <145, pace 6:45–7:15/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk

If knee feels anything, or sleep was bad, do mobility instead.

workouts.name: MP Intervals — 4 x 1 km
workouts.description: Lock in goal marathon pace and rhythm.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15 min easy
Main: 4 x 1 km @ marathon pace (6:55/km, HR 155–165), 2 min jog recovery
Cool-down: 10 min easy

OPTIONAL 4th run — only if you feel fresh.
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 30 min very easy (HR <145, pace 6:45–7:15/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk

If knee feels anything, or sleep was bad, do mobility instead.

workouts.name: Marathon Pace Block — 25 min @ MP
workouts.description: Sustained goal-pace work. Cornerstone of marathon prep.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 15 min easy
Main: 25 min continuous @ marathon pace (6:55/km, HR 155–165)
Cool-down: 10 min easy

workouts.name: Strength A — Lower Body Foundation
workouts.description: Knee insurance. Single-leg & glute work. Non-negotiable now that long runs are growing.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): leg swings, hip circles, glute bridges 2x10
Main (3 rounds, 60s rest):
  • Bodyweight squats — 12 reps (slow & controlled)
  • Reverse lunges — 8 reps each leg
  • Single-leg glute bridges — 10 reps each leg
  • Calf raises — 15 reps
  • Side-lying clamshells — 12 reps each side
Cool-down (5 min): hip flexor & quad stretches

workouts.name: Long Run — 30 km
workouts.description: 🏔️ Peak long run. Easy pace. This is your confidence bank.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk
Main: 30 km easy (7:00–7:30/km, HR 135–150). Deliberately slow.
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretches
Fuel: After 75 min, 30g carbs (gel/banana). Water every 15 min.

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

weeks.focus: Begin taper — cut volume, hold sharpness

workouts.name: Strength B — Core & Stability
workouts.description: Core + single-leg balance for running economy and knee tracking.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up (5 min): cat-cow, bird-dog 2x8
Main (3 rounds, 45s rest):
  • Plank — 30–45s
  • Side plank — 20–30s each side
  • Dead bug — 8 reps each side
  • Single-leg deadlift (no weight) — 8 reps each leg
  • Step-ups onto chair — 10 each leg
Cool-down (5 min): pigeon stretch, child's pose

workouts.name: Easy Run — 45 min
workouts.description: Easy/conversational. Keep HR under 150. This pace should feel comfortable.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + light jog
Main: 35 min easy (HR 135–150, pace 6:30–7:00/km)
Cool-down: 5 min walk + stretch

Cool-down: 10 min easy

workouts.name: Easy + 4 Strides — 25 min
workouts.description: Easy aerobic with short pickups to keep leg speed sharp.
workouts.humanReadable:
Warm-up: 5 min walk + 10 min easy
Main: 0 min easy (6:30–7:00/km)
Strides: 4 x 20s acceleration to ~5K pace on flat, 60s walk between
Cool-down: 5 min walk

workouts.name: Rest Day
workouts.description: Full recovery — fitness is built during rest.
workouts.humanReadable: Sleep, hydrate, eat well. Light walking is fine.

workouts.name: Pre-race shake-out — 15 min
workouts.description: Fire the legs, nothing more.
workouts.humanReadable: 10 min very easy + 4x 20s strides + 5 min walk. Eat well, hydrate, sleep early. Lay out gear tonight.

workouts.name: 🏁 VALENCIA MARATHON 42.2 km
workouts.description: RACE DAY! See Race Strategy tab.
workouts.humanReadable: Goal 6:55/km (~4:51). Negative split: first 10K at 7:05/km, settle into MP. Walk aid stations to fuel. 30g carbs every 30 min from km 8. Trust the build — it's in your legs.

pacing.strategy: Conservative negative split for a first marathon

firstHalf.notes: DELIBERATELY hold back. The first 10 km should feel TOO easy — that's correct.

secondHalf.notes: Reach 30 km feeling fresh, then press slightly. Otherwise hold.

pacing.lastTenK: Whatever's left. Pass people from km 32 on.
pacing.warnings: Your easy pace is quick now, which will tempt you to start fast. Don't. Endurance, not speed, is the unknown in your first marathon.

nutrition.preRace: Night before: pasta + lean protein, nothing new. 3 hr before: 100g carbs (oatmeal+banana+honey, low fiber). 1 cup coffee 60 min before if usual.

during.schedule: Gel at km 8, then every 5 km (8/13/18/23/28/33/37). ~25g carbs each. Walk aid stations to drink cleanly.
during.products: Only what you've practiced in long runs.

nutrition.rule: Nothing new on race day. Lock your fuel protocol in long runs from week 15.

raceMorning.gear: Only what you've trained in. New = blisters.

mentalCues: Km 1–10: 'Easy. So easy. Supposed to feel slow.'
Km 11–21: 'Settle in. Rhythm. Drink and fuel.'
Km 22–32: 'Now the race begins. Stay relaxed.'
Km 33–42: 'Everyone hurts now. The training is in my legs. One km at a time.'

postRace.immediate: Keep walking 10 min. Electrolytes. Eat within 30 min.
postRace.next72hrs: Light walking only. No running. Sleep. Celebrate!
postRace.return: First easy jog 5–7 days after. Two weeks unstructured before structure.

coachingNotes: 📅 REVISION 2 (June 23): Rebuilt to 24 weeks from your current week. You skipped re-base and adapted well, so this starts in Base. Peak (30 km) & 3-week taper unchanged.
📈 Your data: easy pace held at ~6:30/km while HR dropped from 159→139 over 3 weeks. That's real aerobic fitness. Paces recalibrated faster to match.
🏃 THE LONG RUN IS THE JOB NOW. Your runs cluster at 4–6 km; the marathon is won on the Saturday long run. It grows ~1–2 km/week. Keep it SLOWER than your easy runs (7:00–7:30/km).
💪 Strength is your weak link — you said 'occasionally.' Two real sessions a week (Mon + Fri). This is what keeps the knee quiet as long runs pass 15 km. Don't skip it.
🦵 Knee is symptom-free — protect that. The cautious progression that built it still applies: any sharp or persistent pain = scale back immediately, see a physio. Achy/stiff is normal.
😴 Recovery is your real limiter (1yo + work). The Wednesday run is OPTIONAL — skip it freely on bad-sleep weeks. 3 quality runs > 4 tired ones.
🥗 As long runs grow, fuel them: ~250–350g carbs on long-run days. Practice race fuel from week 15.
⌚ Keep exporting from your Watch every few weeks. The HR-at-pace trend is exactly how we'll know if the goal time can come down. Next check-in: after your first 18–20 km run.
🧪 Around week 6, do a 5K time trial (all-out, flat) to validate your tempo/threshold zones — they're still estimated.
